title: Most of the world is covered by 3G and 4G networks. Sub-Saharan Africa is the least connected region
subtitle: Percentage of inhabitants within range of at least a 3G, 4G, or 5G mobile-cellular signal, irrespective of whether they are subscribers
source: International Telecommunication Union, ITU DataHub. Retrieved from Data360 (MOB_COV_3G, MOB_COV_4G, MOB_COV_5G)
visdescription: Three choropleth world maps showing levels of country coverage for at least 3G, 4G, and 5G networks
keyFactLabel: in Sub-Saharan Africa are not covered by a 5G network

scenes.text: 96 percent of the global population lives within reach of at least a  3G network.
Although 4G networks cover over 93 percent of the people in the world, [emphasis: there are large gaps in] [emphasis: low-income economies, mostly in Sub-Saharan Africa, ]particularly in rural areas.

5G is widely available in many high-income economies. In some upper- and lower-middle-income countries like [emphasis: China], [emphasis: India], and [emphasis: Thailand][emphasis: , ]over 80 percent of people are covered by 5G networks.
In Sub-Saharan Africa, the deployment of 5G remains largely confined to a few high-density urban areas, and only [emphasis: Mauritius]and [emphasis: South Africa]have over 50 percent coverage.
